title.zh: 卷八十四 翟方進傳
title.raw: 卷八十四 翟方進傳 Volume 84: Zhai Fangjin

sentences.zh: 卷八十四 翟方進傳第五十四

translations.literal: Volume 84, Biography the Fifty-Fourth: Zhai Fangjin.
translations.idiomatic: Volume 84, the fifty-fourth biography: Zhai Fangjin.

sentences.zh: 翟方進字子威，汝南上蔡人也。

translations.literal: Zhai Fangjin, courtesy name Ziwei, was a man of Shangcai in Runan.
translations.idiomatic: Zhai Fangjin, styled Ziwei, was a native of Shangcai in Runan commandery.

sentences.zh: 家世微賤，至方進父翟公，好學，為郡文學。

translations.literal: His family for generations was mean and low; down to Fangjin's father, Lord Zhai, who loved learning, he became commandery classicist scholar.
translations.idiomatic: The family had long been of humble rank; his father, Lord Zhai, loved books and served as the commandery's erudite instructor.

sentences.zh: 方進年十二三，失父孤學，給事太守府為小史，號遲頓不及事，數為掾史所詈辱。

translations.literal: When Fangjin was twelve or three, he lost his father, studied alone, gave service in the grand warden's headquarters as petty clerk, was called slow and dull and not up to affairs, and several times was reviled and humiliated by the secretaries.
translations.idiomatic: Orphaned at twelve or thirteen, he studied on his own and took a petty clerkship at the grand warden's office, where he was mocked as slow and useless and repeatedly insulted by the senior staff.

sentences.zh: 方進自傷，乃從汝南蔡父相問己能所宜。

translations.literal: Fangjin wounded himself over it, and thereupon followed Elder Cai of Runan to have his physiognomy read as to what his powers suited.
translations.idiomatic: Deeply hurt, he went to an old Mr. Cai in Runan to have his face read and learn what line of work suited him.

sentences.zh: 蔡父大奇其形貌，謂曰：「小史有封侯骨，當以經術進，努力為諸生學問。」

translations.literal: Elder Cai greatly marveled at his form and appearance, saying to him: "The petty clerk has the bone structure of one who will be enfeoffed as marquis; he ought to advance by the classics; exert yourself in the learning of the erudites."
translations.idiomatic: Mr. Cai was struck by his bearing and told him, "A petty clerk you may be, but you have the look of a future marquis. You should rise through the classics—throw yourself into study like a true scholar."

sentences.zh: 方進既厭為小史，聞蔡父言，心喜，因病歸家，辭其後母，欲西至京師受經。

translations.literal: Fangjin already was weary of being petty clerk; hearing Elder Cai's words, his heart rejoiced; because of illness he returned home, took leave of his stepmother, and wished to go west to the capital to receive the classics.
translations.idiomatic: Already sick of clerking, he was elated by Mr. Cai's words. He pleaded illness, went home, asked his stepmother's leave, and resolved to travel west to the capital to study the canon.

sentences.zh: 母憐其幼，隨之長安，織屨以給。

translations.literal: The mother pitied his youth, followed him to Chang'an, and wove sandals to supply him.
translations.idiomatic: His mother, pitying how young he was, followed him to Chang'an and kept them fed by weaving sandals.

sentences.zh: 方進讀經博士，受《春秋》。

translations.literal: Fangjin studied with an erudite over the classics and received the 《Spring and Autumn》.
translations.idiomatic: He enrolled with a court erudite and took the 《Spring and Autumn》 as his text.

sentences.zh: 積十餘年，經學明習，徒眾日廣，諸儒稱之。

translations.literal: Accumulating more than ten years, his classical learning was clear and practiced, his disciples daily broader, and the various scholars praised him.
translations.idiomatic: After more than a decade his mastery of the classics was evident, his following grew by the day, and other scholars spoke of him with respect.

sentences.zh: 以射策甲科為郎。

translations.literal: By archery-examination in the first class he became gentleman.
translations.idiomatic: He placed in the top tier of the written examination and was appointed a gentleman of the palace.

sentences.zh: 二三歲，舉明經，遷議郎。

translations.literal: In two or three years he was recommended for understanding the classics and was transferred to advisory gentleman.
translations.idiomatic: Within two or three years he was nominated on the "illustrious classics" roster and promoted to advisory gentleman.

sentences.zh: 是時，宿儒有清河胡常，與方進同經。

translations.literal: At this time among the long-standing scholars was Hu Chang of Qinghe, who shared the same classic with Fangjin.
translations.idiomatic: Among the senior scholars of the day was Hu Chang of Qinghe, who studied the same classic as Fangjin.

sentences.zh: 常為先進，名譽出方進下，心害其能，論議不右方進。

translations.literal: Chang was the earlier advanced one, but his fame and reputation came out below Fangjin's; his heart envied Fangjin's ability, and in discussion he did not support Fangjin.
translations.idiomatic: Hu had entered the field first, yet Fangjin's reputation soon eclipsed his; resentful of the younger man's talent, he would never speak up for him in debate.

sentences.zh: 方進知之，候伺常大都授時，遣門下諸生至常所問大義疑難，因記其說。

translations.literal: Fangjin knew it; he waited until when Chang was giving his great assembly lecture, sent the various students of his gate to Chang's place to ask about great meanings and doubtful difficulties, and thereby recorded his explanations.
translations.idiomatic: Fangjin waited until Hu gave his large public lecture, then sent his own students to Hu's hall with knotty questions on the text—and quietly took notes on every answer.

sentences.zh: 如是者久之，常知方進之宗讓己，內不自得，其後居士大夫之間未嘗不稱述方進，遂相親友。

translations.literal: Having been thus for a long time, Chang knew Fangjin's purpose was to yield to himself; inwardly he was not at ease; afterward, dwelling among great officers, he never failed to praise and recount Fangjin, and they thereupon became intimate friends.
translations.idiomatic: This went on until Hu realized Fangjin was deliberately deferring to him; ashamed, he thereafter never missed a chance among officials to praise Fangjin, and the two became close friends.

sentences.zh: 河平中，方進轉為博士。

translations.literal: In the Heping period, Fangjin was transferred to erudite.
translations.idiomatic: During the Heping era he was rotated into a court erudite post.

sentences.zh: 數年，遷朔方刺史，居官不煩苛，所察應條輒舉，甚有威名。

translations.literal: After several years he was transferred to regional inspector of Shuofang; in office he was not vexing and harsh; whatever he inspected that matched the statutes he always reported, and he had very great awe-inspiring name.
translations.idiomatic: A few years later he was made regional inspector of Shuofang. He governed without petty harshness, cited every breach of statute he found, and quickly earned a formidable reputation.

sentences.zh: 再三奏事，遷為丞相司直。

translations.literal: Again and again he memorialized on affairs and was transferred to be rectifier for the chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: Repeated memorials on his work won him promotion to the chancellor's rectifier, the chief disciplinary officer under the chief minister.

sentences.zh: 從上甘泉，行馳道中，司隸校尉陳慶劾奏方進，沒入車馬。

translations.literal: Following the emperor up to Sweet Springs, he traveled in the expressway; Metropolitan Commandant Chen Qing impeached and memorialized Fangjin, and his carriage and horses were confiscated.
translations.idiomatic: While accompanying the emperor to Sweet Springs he drove on the imperial express lane; Metropolitan Commandant Chen Qing impeached him, and his team was confiscated.

sentences.zh: 既至甘泉宮，會殿中，慶與廷尉范延壽語，時慶有章劾，自道：「行事以贖論，今尚書持我事來，當於此決。

translations.literal: Having arrived at Sweet Springs Palace, meeting in the hall, Qing spoke with Commandant of Justice Fan Yanshou; at the time Qing had a memorial impeachment; he himself said: "The conduct of the affair is to be judged by ransom assessment; now the secretariat is holding my affair here; it ought to be decided here.
translations.idiomatic: At Sweet Springs Palace, during a hall assembly, Qing was talking with Commandant of Justice Fan Yanshou. Under investigation himself, Qing said aloud, "The case was supposed to be settled by commutation; now the secretariat has brought my file—I expect the decision here.

sentences.zh: 前我為尚書時，嘗有所奏事，忽忘之，留月餘。」

translations.literal: Formerly when I was secretariat director, I once had something I memorialized on, suddenly forgot it, and left it more than a month."
translations.idiomatic: When I was at the secretariat I once drafted a memorial, forgot it in a moment, and let it sit for over a month."

sentences.zh: 方進於是舉劾慶曰：「案慶奉使刺舉大臣，故為尚書，知機事周密一統，明主躬親不解。

translations.literal: Fangjin thereupon raised impeachment of Qing, saying: "We investigate: Qing received commission to investigate and report on great ministers; formerly he was secretariat director, knew that the machinery of affairs is tight and unified, and the enlightened ruler personally attends without slack.
translations.idiomatic: Fangjin impeached him: "Qing was charged with investigating high officials and once headed the secretariat; he knew state business is centralized and that the sovereign attends to it personally without respite.

sentences.zh: 慶有罪未伏誅，無恐懼心，豫自設不坐之比。

translations.literal: Qing has guilt and has not submitted to execution; he has no heart of fear and dread; beforehand he himself set up analogies by which he would not sit guilty.
translations.idiomatic: Though liable to punishment he shows no fear, and he has already framed arguments to exculpate himself.

sentences.zh: 又暴揚尚書事，言遲疾無所在，虧損聖德之聰明，奉詔不謹，皆不敬，臣謹以劾。」

translations.literal: Moreover he violently exposed and broadcast secretariat affairs, speaking of delay and speed as having no fixed place, injuring and diminishing the sagely virtue's intelligence and clarity; receiving edicts was not careful—all is irreverence; your servant respectfully thereby impeaches."
translations.idiomatic: He also aired confidential secretariat business, dismissed timing as arbitrary, and impugned the throne's judgment—all grave disrespect to imperial orders. I therefore impeach him."

sentences.zh: 慶坐免官。

translations.literal: Qing by sitting lost office.
translations.idiomatic: Qing was stripped of his post on those charges.

sentences.zh: 會北地浩商為義渠長所捕，亡，長取其母，與豭豬連繫都亭下。

translations.literal: It happened that Hao Shang of Beidi was arrested by the chief of Yiqu; he fled; the chief took his mother and together with a boar pig chained her under the metropolitan relay station.
translations.idiomatic: Hao Shang of Beidi was seized by the magistrate of Yiqu but escaped; the magistrate then took Shang's mother, chained her beside a boar under the county relay post, and left her in the street.

sentences.zh: 商兄弟會賓客，自稱司隸掾、長安縣尉，殺義渠長妻子六人，亡。

translations.literal: Shang's brothers assembled guests, called themselves metropolitan commandant clerk and Chang'an county lieutenant, killed the Yiqu chief's wife and children six persons, and fled.
translations.idiomatic: Shang's brothers gathered retainers, posed as metropolitan staff and a Chang'an deputy, murdered the magistrate's wife and children—six people in all—and fled.

sentences.zh: 丞相、御史請遣掾史與司隸校尉、部刺史並力逐捕，察無狀者，奏可。

translations.literal: The chancellor and imperial counselor requested to send clerks together with the metropolitan commandant and regional inspectors to join strength in pursuit and arrest, and to investigate those without evidence; the memorial was approved.
translations.idiomatic: The chancellor and imperial counselor asked that their clerks join the metropolitan commandant and regional inspectors in a joint manhunt, with a review of anyone wrongly suspected; the emperor approved.

sentences.zh: 司隸校尉涓勳奏言：「《春秋》之義，王人微者序乎諸侯之上，尊王命也。

translations.literal: Metropolitan Commandant Juan Xun memorialized, saying: "By the meaning of the 《Spring and Autumn》, the king's man though humble is ordered above the feudal lords—this is to honor the king's command.
translations.idiomatic: Metropolitan Commandant Juan Xun wrote: "The 《Spring and Autumn》 ranks even a minor envoy of the king above feudal lords—to honor the royal mandate.

sentences.zh: 臣幸得奉使，以督察公卿以下為職，今丞相宣請遣掾史，以宰士督察天子奉使命大夫，甚悖逆順之理。

translations.literal: Your servant fortunately was able to receive commission, taking as duty to supervise and inspect from the dukes and ministers downward; now Chancellor Xuan requests to send clerks, by which ministerial clerks supervise and inspect great officers whom the Son of Heaven commissioned with mission—this greatly violates the principle of what is contrary and what is compliant.
translations.idiomatic: I was charged with overseeing officials down from the highest ranks; now Chancellor Xuan wants to dispatch his own clerks to supervise officers commissioned by the throne—an inversion of proper order that flatly contradicts the classics.

sentences.zh: 宣本不師受經術，因事以立奸威，案浩商所犯，一家之禍耳，而宣欲專權作威，乃害於國，不可之大者。

translations.literal: Xuan fundamentally did not study and receive classical learning; by affairs he established wicked awesomeness; investigating what Hao Shang committed, it is the misfortune of one household only, yet Xuan wishes to monopolize power and make awesomeness—then it harms the state; it is of the greatest what cannot be.
translations.idiomatic: Xuan never trained in the classics; he exploits incidents to build arbitrary power. Hao Shang's crime was a private family tragedy, yet the chancellor would monopolize authority and throw his weight around—an intolerable injury to the state.

sentences.zh: 願下中朝特進列侯、將軍以下，正國法度。」

translations.literal: I wish to send down to the inner court specially advanced full marquises, from generals downward, to rectify the state's laws and measures."
translations.idiomatic: I ask that the matter be referred to the inner court—full marquises, generals, and those below—to restore correct statutory order."

sentences.zh: 議者以為，丞相掾不宜移書皆趣司隸。

translations.literal: Those who deliberated thought that chancellor clerks ought not to circulate documents all pressing the metropolitan commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Critics held that chancellery clerks should not blanket the metropolitan command with circular orders.

sentences.zh: 會浩商捕得伏誅，家屬徙合浦。

translations.literal: It happened that Hao Shang was captured, submitted to execution, and his family dependents were moved to Hepu.
translations.idiomatic: Hao Shang was soon caught and executed; his kin were banished to Hepu.

sentences.zh: 故事，司隸校尉位在司直下，初除，謁兩府，其有所會，居中二千石前，與司直並迎丞相、御史。

translations.literal: By precedent, the metropolitan commandant's rank was below the rectifier's; on first appointment he paid visit to the two offices; when there was meeting, he stood in the center before the two-thousand-bushel ranks, and together with the rectifier jointly welcomed the chancellor and imperial counselor.
translations.idiomatic: By established usage the metropolitan commandant ranked below the chancellor's rectifier, called on both chancellery and counselor's offices when appointed, took the center place ahead of other two-thousand-bushel officials at joint sessions, and with the rectifier greeted the chancellor and imperial counselor.

sentences.zh: 初，方進新視事，而涓勳亦初拜為司隸，不肯謁丞相、御史大夫，後朝會相見，禮節又倨。

translations.literal: At first, Fangjin had newly taken office, and Juan Xun also had been newly appointed metropolitan commandant; he was unwilling to pay visit to the chancellor and imperial counselor; afterward at court meeting when they saw each other, the ritual demeanor was moreover arrogant.
translations.idiomatic: When Fangjin had just assumed his post, Juan Xun had also just been named metropolitan commandant; Xun refused to call on the chancellor or imperial counselor, and when they later met at court his bearing was openly disrespectful.

sentences.zh: 方進陰察之，勳私過光祿勳辛慶忌，又出逢帝舅成都侯商道路，下車立，□過，乃就車。

translations.literal: Fangjin secretly investigated him; Xun privately visited Superintendent of the Household Xin Qingji; moreover going out he met on the road the emperor's maternal uncle Marquis of Chengdu Wang Shang, dismounted the carriage and stood until Shang had passed, then mounted the carriage.
translations.idiomatic: Fangjin had him watched: Xun paid a private call on Superintendent of the Household Xin Qingji, and another time, meeting the emperor's uncle, Marquis of Chengdu Wang Shang, on the road, he dismounted and stood deferentially until Shang had passed before remounting.

sentences.zh: 於是方進舉奏其狀，因曰：「臣聞國家之興，尊尊而敬長，爵位上下之禮，王道綱紀。

translations.literal: Thereupon Fangjin raised and memorialized his condition, saying thereby: "Your servant has heard that in the rise of the state, honoring the honored and respecting the elders, the ritual of rank high and low, is the kingly way's guideline and cord.
translations.idiomatic: Fangjin reported the facts and declared: "I have always understood that a thriving polity rests on honoring superiors and elders and on clear precedence among ranks—the very fabric of kingship.

sentences.zh: 《春秋》之義，尊上公謂之宰，海內無不統焉。

translations.literal: By the meaning of the 《Spring and Autumn》, honoring the high duke is called steward; within the seas there is none he does not unify.
translations.idiomatic: The 《Spring and Autumn》 teaches us to call the chief minister steward: nothing within the realm lies outside his coordinating hand.

sentences.zh: 丞相進見聖主，御坐為起，在輿為下。

translations.literal: When the chancellor advances to see the sage lord, for the imperial seat he rises; in the carriage he descends.
translations.idiomatic: When the chancellor is admitted to audience, the sovereign rises from the throne and steps down from his chariot to show respect.

sentences.zh: 群臣宜皆承順聖化，以視四方。

translations.literal: The various ministers ought all to receive and comply with the sage transformation, so as to show it to the four directions.
translations.idiomatic: Every minister should embody that civilizing example and hold it up for the empire to see.

sentences.zh: 勳吏二千石，幸得奉使，不遵禮儀，輕謾宰相，賤易上卿，而又詘節失度，邪諂無常，色厲內荏。

translations.literal: Xun is official of two-thousand-bushel rank, fortunately was able to receive commission, does not comply with ritual and ceremony, lightly insults the chancellor, treats cheaply and lightly the high minister, and moreover bends the tally and loses measure, is wicked flattery without constancy, fierce in appearance but soft within.
translations.idiomatic: Xun holds a two-thousand-bushel commission, yet he spurns protocol, sneers at the chief minister, treats senior ministers with contempt, wavers between cringing and bluster, and is all show—fierce in face, timid at heart.

sentences.zh: 墮國體，亂朝廷之序，不宜處位。

translations.literal: He casts down the state's body, disorders the court's sequence; he is not suitable to occupy position.
translations.idiomatic: He degrades the dignity of government and scrambles court precedence; he should not remain in office.

sentences.zh: 臣請下丞相免勳。」

translations.literal: Your servant requests to send down to the chancellor to dismiss Xun."
translations.idiomatic: I therefore ask that the chancellor be directed to remove Juan Xun from his post.

sentences.zh: 時，太中大夫平當給事中奏言：「方進國之司直，不自敕正以先群下，前親犯令行馳道中，司隸慶平心舉劾，方進不自責悔而內挾私恨，伺記慶之從容語言，以詆欺成罪。

translations.literal: At the time, Grandee of the Palace Ping Dang, as palace attendant, memorialized, saying: "Fangjin is the state's rectifier; he does not himself correct himself to lead the various subordinates; formerly he personally violated the ordinance by traveling in the expressway; Metropolitan Commandant Qing with level mind raised impeachment; Fangjin did not himself blame and repent but inwardly harbored private hatred, spied and recorded Qing's leisurely words, and by slander and deceit completed guilt.
translations.idiomatic: Grandee of the Palace Ping Dang, serving as palace attendant, objected: "Fangjin is the empire's chief censor, yet he did not set the example: he himself broke the law on the express lane. Chen Qing impeached him fairly, but instead of accepting blame Fangjin nursed a grudge, eavesdropped on Qing's casual remarks, and twisted them into a criminal charge."

sentences.zh: 後丞相宣以一不道賊，請遣掾督趣司隸校尉，司隸校尉勳自奏暴於朝廷，今方進復舉奏勳。

translations.literal: Afterward Chancellor Xuan because of one non-way bandit requested to send clerks to supervise and press the metropolitan commandant; Metropolitan Commandant Xun himself memorialized exposing it at the court; now Fangjin again raises impeachment of Xun.
translations.idiomatic: Later Chancellor Xuan, over a single criminal case, ordered his clerks to hound the metropolitan commandant; Commandant Xun appealed openly in court, and now Fangjin impeaches him again.

sentences.zh: 議者以為方進不以道德輔正丞相，苟阿助大臣，欲必勝立威，宜抑絕其原。

translations.literal: Those who deliberated thought that Fangjin did not by virtue and the Way assist and correct the chancellor, carelessly curried and aided great ministers, wished by necessity to win and establish awesomeness; it was fitting to suppress and cut off his source.
translations.idiomatic: Critics argued that Fangjin was not guiding the chancellor with moral authority but currying favor with powerful colleagues, seeking only to win every clash and build a reputation for fear; the root of that habit ought to be checked.

sentences.zh: 勳素行公直，奸人所惡，可少寬假，使遂其功名。」

translations.literal: Xun by nature in conduct was public and straight, what wicked men hate; one may slightly grant lenience, and let him complete his merit and name."
translations.idiomatic: Xun's record is upright—the very sort of man villains detest. Grant him a little latitude and let his career stand on its merits.

sentences.zh: 上以方進所舉應科，不得用逆詐廢正法，遂貶勳為昌陵令。

translations.literal: The emperor considered that what Fangjin raised matched the statutes; one could not by reverse deceit discard correct law; thereupon he demoted Xun to be magistrate of Changling.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor ruled that Fangjin's charges met the code and that the law could not be set aside for suspicion of bad faith; Xun was therefore demoted to magistrate of Changling.

sentences.zh: 方進旬歲間免兩司隸，朝廷由是憚之。

translations.literal: Within a year Fangjin dismissed two metropolitan commandants; the court by this feared him.
translations.idiomatic: In the space of a year Fangjin had forced out two metropolitan commandants, and the whole bureaucracy learned to fear him.

sentences.zh: 丞相宣甚器重焉，常誡掾史：「謹事司直，翟君必在相位，不久。」

translations.literal: Chancellor Xuan greatly valued him, often warning the clerks: "Serve the rectifier carefully; Lord Zhai will certainly be in chancellor position before long."
translations.idiomatic: Chancellor Xuan thought the world of him and would warn his staff, "Treat the rectifier with care—Master Zhai will sit in the chief ministership before you know it."

sentences.zh: 是時，起昌陵，營作陵邑，貴戚近臣子弟賓客多辜榷為奸利者，方進部掾史復案，發大奸贓數千萬。

translations.literal: At this time they were raising Changling, constructing works for the tomb district; among the noble kin, near ministers' sons and younger brothers and guests, many monopolized by extortion and made wicked profit; Fangjin's department clerks again investigated, exposing great wickedness and embezzlement to the count of several tens of millions.
translations.idiomatic: While the Changling mausoleum project was under way, well-connected families and their hangers-on were monopolizing supplies and raking in illicit millions; Fangjin's investigators reopened the files and uncovered fraud on a staggering scale.

sentences.zh: 上以為任公卿，欲試以治民，徙方進為京兆尹，搏擊豪強，京師畏之。

translations.literal: The emperor considered him fit to be entrusted with dukes and ministers, wished to try him in governing the people, transferred Fangjin to be metropolitan governor of the capital, struck and attacked powerful magnates, and the capital feared him.
translations.idiomatic: Judging him ready for higher responsibility, the emperor wanted to test his skill with the populace and named him metropolitan governor of Jingzhao. He cracked down on powerful local clans, and the capital trembled.

sentences.zh: 時，胡常為青州刺史，聞之，與方進書曰：「竊聞政令甚明，為京兆能，則恐有所不宜。」

translations.literal: At the time Hu Chang was regional inspector of Qingzhou; hearing it, he sent Fangjin a letter saying: "I have heard your government orders are very clear; as metropolitan governor you are capable—then I fear there may be something not suitable."
translations.idiomatic: Hu Chang, then regional inspector of Qingzhou, wrote to him: "Your administration is said to be impeccably strict. In the capital that may prove a mixed blessing."

sentences.zh: 方進心知所謂，其後少弛威嚴。

translations.literal: Fangjin in his heart knew what was meant; afterward he slightly relaxed awe and severity.
translations.idiomatic: Fangjin took the hint and thereafter eased off a little of his harsh edge.

sentences.zh: 居官三歲，永始二年遷御史大夫。

translations.literal: Having been in office three years, in the second year of Yongshi he was transferred to imperial counselor.
translations.idiomatic: After three years in post he was promoted to imperial counselor in the second year of Yongshi.

sentences.zh: 數月，會丞相薛宣坐廣漢盜賊群起及太皇太后喪時三輔吏並征發為奸，免為庶人。

translations.literal: After several months, it happened that Chancellor Xue Xuan sat guilty because in Guanghan bandits rose in groups and at the Grand Empress Dowager's funeral the capital region officers together in conscripting labor committed wickedness; he was dismissed to be commoner.
translations.idiomatic: A few months later Chancellor Xue Xuan was stripped of rank—blamed for the Guanghan bandit uprising and for corruption among metropolitan officials during the Grand Empress Dowager's funeral levies.

sentences.zh: 方進亦坐為京兆尹時奉喪事煩擾百姓，左遷執金吾。

translations.literal: Fangjin also sat because when metropolitan governor in conducting funeral affairs he troubled and vexed the hundred surnames; he was demoted left to superintendent of the capital.
translations.idiomatic: Fangjin too was censured for harassing the people while metropolitan governor during the same funeral round; he was demoted to superintendent of the capital.

sentences.zh: 二十餘日，丞相官缺，群臣多舉方進，上亦器其能，遂擢方進為丞相，封高陵侯，食邑千戶。

translations.literal: After more than twenty days the chancellor office was vacant; the various ministers mostly recommended Fangjin; the emperor also valued his ability; thereupon he promoted Fangjin to be chancellor, enfeoffed him as marquis of Gaoling, with fief of one thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: Within three weeks the chief ministership was open again. Most of the court nominated Fangjin, and the emperor, confident in his talent, elevated him to chancellor with a marquisate at Gaoling and a thousand-household fief.

sentences.zh: 身既富貴，而後母尚在，方進內行修飾，供養甚篤。

translations.literal: His person already being rich and noble, while the stepmother still lived, Fangjin in inner conduct cultivated and adorned himself, and supplied and supported her very earnestly.
translations.idiomatic: Once he was wealthy and powerful, his stepmother still lived; he kept his private life above reproach and supported her with conspicuous devotion.

sentences.zh: 及後母終，既葬三十六日，除服起視事，以為身備漢相，不敢逾國家之制。

translations.literal: When the stepmother ended, after burial thirty-six days, he removed mourning and rose to attend to affairs, considering that his person filled the Han chancellorship, not daring to transgress the state's regulations.
translations.idiomatic: When she died he buried her, observed only thirty-six days of mourning, then doffed sackcloth and returned to duty—holding that as Han chief minister he must not exceed statutory mourning.

sentences.zh: 為相公潔，請托不行郡國。

translations.literal: As chancellor he was public and clean; requests and entreaties did not go through in commanderies and kingdoms.
translations.idiomatic: As chief minister he kept a spotless public reputation: no favor-seeking lobby ever got a hearing in the provinces.

sentences.zh: 持法刻深，舉奏牧守九卿，峻文深詆，中傷者尤多。

translations.literal: He held the law carved and deep; he raised and memorialized shepherds, guards, and the nine ministers; with harsh wording and deep slander, those struck in the middle were especially many.
translations.idiomatic: He enforced the code with pitiless rigor, impeaching governors and ministers alike with harsh indictments, and ruined a great many careers.

sentences.zh: 如陳咸、硃博、蕭育、逢信、孫閎之屬，皆京師世家，以材能少歷牧守列卿，知名當世，而方進特立後起，十餘年間至宰相，據法以彈咸等，皆罷退之。

translations.literal: Such as Chen Xian, Zhu Bo, Xiao Yu, Feng Xin, Sun Hong and the like—all were capital region hereditary houses, by talent and ability in youth had passed through shepherds, guards, and ranked ministers, were famous in the age, yet Fangjin stood alone as later riser; within more than ten years he reached chancellor; relying on the law he impeached Xian and the others; all were dismissed and retired.
translations.idiomatic: Men like Chen Xian, Zhu Bo, Xiao Yu, Feng Xin, and Sun Hong were old capital families who had risen young to governorships and ministerial posts and enjoyed national renown—yet Fangjin, a latecomer who reached the chancellorship in little more than a decade, used the statutes to impeach them and had every one removed.

sentences.zh: 後二歲餘，詔舉方正直言之士，紅陽侯立舉咸對策，拜為光祿大夫給事中。

translations.literal: After more than two years, an edict ordered recommendation of men of square integrity who spoke directly; Marquis of Hongyang Wang Li recommended Xian for policy response, and he was appointed grand counselor of the palace with palace attendant status.
translations.idiomatic: More than two years later the court called for candid critics; Marquis of Hongyang Wang Li nominated Chen Xian, who passed the policy examination and was named grand counselor of the palace with concurrent palace attendant duties.

sentences.zh: 方進復奏：「咸前為九卿，坐為貪邪免，自知罪惡暴陳，依托紅陽侯立徼幸，有司莫敢舉奏。

translations.literal: Fangjin again memorialized: "Xian formerly was one of the nine ministers; he sat because of greed and wickedness and was dismissed; he himself knew his guilt and wickedness were openly displayed; he relied on Marquis of Hongyang Wang Li for lucky chance; the responsible offices none dared raise impeachment.
translations.idiomatic: Fangjin shot back: "Chen Xian once sat among the nine ministers until greed cost him his post. His crimes were a matter of record, yet he clung to Wang Li for another chance, and no agency dared touch him."

sentences.zh: 冒濁苟容，不顧恥辱，不當蒙方正舉，備內朝臣。」

translations.literal: He braved turbidity and endured disgrace, did not regard shame and dishonor; he ought not to receive square integrity recommendation and fill inner-court minister."
translations.idiomatic: He wallowed in corruption and traded dignity for survival; he does not deserve a "square and upright" nomination or a seat among the inner court.

sentences.zh: 並劾紅陽侯立選舉故不以實。

translations.literal: Together he impeached Marquis of Hongyang Wang Li for recommendation's reason not according with fact.
translations.idiomatic: He also impeached Wang Li for a knowingly false nomination.

sentences.zh: 有詔免咸，勿劾立。

translations.literal: There was an edict to dismiss Xian, not to impeach Li.
translations.idiomatic: An edict dismissed Chen Xian but forbade action against Wang Li.

sentences.zh: 後數年，皇太后姊子侍中衛尉定陵侯淳于長有罪，上以太后故，免官勿治罪。

translations.literal: After several years, the Empress Dowager's elder sister's son, attendant-in-chief and commandant of the guards Marquis of Dingling Chunyu Chang, had guilt; because of the empress dowager the emperor dismissed him from office and did not punish the crime.
translations.idiomatic: Some years later Chunyu Chang—nephew of the empress dowager, Marquis of Dingling, and superintendent of the guards—was found guilty, but the emperor, for his mother's sake, merely removed him from office without prosecution.

sentences.zh: 有司奏請遣長就國，長以金錢與立，立上封事為長求留曰：「陛下既托文以皇太后故，誠不可更有它計。」

translations.literal: The responsible offices memorialized requesting to send Chang to his state; Chang gave gold and cash to Li; Li submitted a sealed memorial for Chang seeking to be retained, saying: "Your Majesty already has entrusted the text because of the empress dowager; truly one cannot again have other plan."
translations.idiomatic: Officials asked that Chang be sent to his fief; Chang bribed Wang Li, who filed a sealed plea begging the throne to let him stay, arguing: "Since Your Majesty already waived prosecution for the empress dowager's sake, there must be no second ruling."

sentences.zh: 後長陰事發，遂下獄。

translations.literal: Afterward Chang's hidden affairs broke out; thereupon he was cast into prison.
translations.idiomatic: When Chang's secret dealings surfaced, he was thrown into prison.

sentences.zh: 方進劾立：「懷奸邪，亂朝政，欲傾誤要主上，狡猾不道，請下獄。」

translations.literal: Fangjin impeached Li: "He harbors wickedness and perversity, disorders court government, wishes to overturn and mislead and importune the lord above; he is crafty and not in the Way; I request to cast him into prison."
translations.idiomatic: Fangjin impeached Wang Li: "He harbors treacherous designs, disrupts the government, and importunes the throne with crafty impiety—send him to prison."

sentences.zh: 上曰：「紅陽侯，朕之舅，不忍致法，遣就國。」

translations.literal: The emperor said: "Marquis of Hongyang is my maternal uncle; I cannot bear to apply the law; send him to his state."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor replied, "Hongyang is my uncle; I cannot bring myself to try him—banish him to his fief instead."

sentences.zh: 於是方進復奏立黨友曰：「立素行積為不善，眾人所共知。

translations.literal: Thereupon Fangjin again memorialized Li's faction and friends, saying: "Li's plain conduct accumulated as not good; it is what the multitude together knows.
translations.idiomatic: Fangjin followed with a memorial on Wang Li's clique: "Li's long record of misconduct is common knowledge.

sentences.zh: 邪臣自結，附托為黨，庶幾立與政事，欲獲其利。

translations.literal: Wicked ministers on their own knot together, attach and rely to form faction, hoping perhaps that Li may join government affairs, wishing to obtain their profit.
translations.idiomatic: Corrupt officials have banded together around him, hoping he would regain influence and they would profit.

sentences.zh: 今立斥還就國，所交結尤著者，不宜備大臣，為郡守。

translations.literal: Now Li is expelled and returned to his state; those he associated and knotted with who are especially manifest ought not to fill great ministers or be commandery governors.
translations.idiomatic: Now that Li has been packed off to his fief, his closest associates must not remain as senior ministers or provincial governors.

sentences.zh: 案後將軍硃博、巨鹿太守孫閎、故光祿大夫陳咸與立交通厚善，相與為腹心，有背公死黨之信，欲相攀援，死而後已；

translations.literal: We investigate: General of the Rear Zhu Bo, Julu grand warden Sun Hong, and former grand counselor of the palace Chen Xian with Li had intercourse thick and good, mutually acted as heart and belly, had faith of betraying the public and dying-for-the-clique, wished mutually to climb and aid, until death and afterward stopping;
translations.idiomatic: General of the Rear Zhu Bo, Julu Governor Sun Hong, and former Grand Counselor Chen Xian were thick with Li—his confidants, sworn to private loyalty, ready to climb together or fall together.

sentences.zh: 皆內有不仁之性，而外有俊材，過絕人倫，勇猛果敢，處事不疑，所居皆尚殘賊酷虐，苛刻慘毒以立威，而無纖介愛利之風。

translations.literal: All inwardly have the nature of not benevolent, yet outwardly have outstanding talent, surpassing and cutting off human relations, brave and fierce and decisive, in handling affairs without doubt; wherever they dwell all esteem cruel banditry and harsh brutality, harsh and carved and cruel and poisonous to establish awesomeness, yet have not the wind of the slightest love of benefit.
translations.idiomatic: Inwardly they lack humaneness, yet they are formidably gifted—bold, ruthless, and decisive. Every post they held was governed by cruelty and terror, never by the slightest regard for the common good.

sentences.zh: 天下所共知，愚者猶惑。

translations.literal: What the world together knows, even fools still are deluded.
translations.idiomatic: The whole empire knows it, though the slow-witted may still be fooled.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：『人而不仁如禮何！

translations.literal: Confucius said: "If a man is not benevolent, what of ritual!
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said, "What has the inhumane man to do with ritual!"

sentences.zh: 人而不仁如樂何！」

translations.literal: If a man is not benevolent, what of music!"
translations.idiomatic: What has he to do with music!"

sentences.zh: 言不仁之人，亡所施用；

translations.literal: It means the not-benevolent man has nowhere to apply and use;
translations.idiomatic: The point is that the inhumane man is fit for nothing.

sentences.zh: 不仁而多材，國之患也。

translations.literal: Not benevolent yet much talent is the state's calamity.
translations.idiomatic: Talent without humaneness is a scourge on the state.

sentences.zh: 此三人皆內懷奸猾，國之所患，而深相與結，信於貴戚奸臣，此國家大憂，大臣所宜沒身而爭也。

translations.literal: These three men all inwardly harbor wicked craftiness, what the state worries about, yet deeply mutually knot together, trusted by noble kin and wicked ministers—this is the state's great anxiety, what great ministers ought to exhaust body contending over.
translations.idiomatic: These three are inwardly treacherous—precisely the sort of men who endanger the state—yet they are tightly bound to powerful in-laws and corrupt favorites. That is a grave peril, and it is a minister's duty to stake his life opposing it.

sentences.zh: 昔季孫行父有害曰：『見有善於君者愛之，若孝子之養父母也；

translations.literal: Formerly Jisun Xingfu had a maxim, saying: "When one sees one who has goodness toward the ruler, love him, as a filial son's nourishing parents;
translations.idiomatic: Ji Sun Xingfu once said, "When you see a man who serves the ruler well, cherish him as a dutiful son cherishes his parents;

sentences.zh: 見不善者誅之，若鷹鸇之逐鳥爵也。』

translations.literal: when one sees one who is not good, punish him, as hawk and osprey pursue birds and sparrows."
translations.idiomatic: when you see a villain, strike him down as a hawk strikes small birds."

sentences.zh: 翅翼雖傷，不避也。

translations.literal: Though wings are injured, he does not avoid.
translations.idiomatic: Even if it costs you your wings, you do not flinch."

sentences.zh: 貴戚強黨之眾誠難犯，犯之，眾敵並怨，善惡相冒。

translations.literal: The crowd of noble kin and strong faction is truly hard to offend; offending them, many foes together resent, good and evil mutually overlap.
translations.idiomatic: Powerful cliques of in-laws are dangerous to cross—cross them and a host of enemies will howl, until right and wrong are hopelessly tangled.

sentences.zh: 臣幸得備宰相，不敢不盡死。

translations.literal: Your servant fortunately was able to serve as chancellor; I dare not fail to give my life.
translations.idiomatic: I am privileged to stand as chancellor; I cannot shrink from giving my life if need be.

sentences.zh: 請免博、閎、咸歸故郡，以銷奸雄之黨，絕群邪之望。」

translations.literal: I request to dismiss Bo, Hong, and Xian to return to their former commanderies, so as to melt away the faction of treacherous heroes and cut off the hope of the crowd of wicked."
translations.idiomatic: I ask that Zhu Bo, Sun Hong, and Chen Xian be stripped of office and sent home to their native commanderies, to break the back of this cabal and end the hopes of every schemer."

sentences.zh: 奏可。

translations.literal: The memorial was approved.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor approved the memorial.

sentences.zh: 咸既廢錮，復徙故郡，以憂死。

translations.literal: Xian already was cast aside and barred, again was moved to former commandery, and died of worry.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Xian was disgraced and barred from office, banished again to his home commandery, and died of grief.

sentences.zh: 方進知能有餘，兼通文法吏事，以儒雅緣飭法律，號為通明相，天子甚器重之，奏事亡不當意，內求人主微指以固其位。

translations.literal: Fangjin's knowledge and ability had surplus; he also comprehended documentary law and clerk affairs; by Confucian refinement he trimmed and adorned the laws; he was called the perspicuous-through chancellor; the Son of Heaven greatly valued him; on memorialized affairs none failed to match intention; inwardly he sought the ruler's subtle pointers to firm his position.
translations.idiomatic: Fangjin had talent to spare and knew the statutes as well as any clerk; he draped harsh law in Confucian polish and was known as the all-knowing chief minister. The emperor leaned on him heavily—every memorial hit the mark—while Fangjin read the sovereign's slightest mood to secure his own standing.

sentences.zh: 初，定陵侯淳于長雖外戚，然以能謀議為九卿，新用事，方進獨與長交，稱薦之。

translations.literal: At first, Marquis of Dingling Chunyu Chang though was outer kin, yet by ability in planning and deliberation became one of the nine ministers, newly wielded power; Fangjin alone associated with Chang and praised and recommended him.
translations.idiomatic: Early on, Marquis of Dingling Chunyu Chang—though only an in-law—had won a seat among the nine ministers on sheer political savvy. Fangjin alone befriended him at the height of his influence and spoke well of him at court.

sentences.zh: 及長坐大逆誅，諸所厚善皆坐長免，上以方進大臣，又素重之，為隱諱。

translations.literal: When Chang sat for great treason and was executed, all whom he had thickly befriended all sat because of Chang and were dismissed; the emperor because Fangjin was great minister, and also had long valued him, for him concealed and covered.
translations.idiomatic: When Chang was executed for high treason, everyone who had been close to him lost office too, but the emperor shielded Fangjin—he was a senior minister whom the throne had long esteemed.

sentences.zh: 方進內慚，上疏謝罪乞骸骨。

translations.literal: Fangjin inwardly was ashamed; he submitted a memorial apologizing for guilt and begging for bones and flesh.
translations.idiomatic: Fangjin was mortified and offered a memorial confessing fault and asking to retire.

sentences.zh: 上報曰：「定陵侯長已伏其辜，君雖交通，傳不雲乎？

translations.literal: The emperor replied, saying: "Marquis of Dingling Chang already has submitted to his guilt; though you had intercourse, does the transmission not say?
translations.idiomatic: The emperor answered: "Chang has paid for his crimes. True, you kept company with him, but has it not been said?

sentences.zh: 『朝過夕改，君子與之』，君何疑焉？

translations.literal: 'If in the morning there is fault, in the evening he reforms—the gentleman approves of him'—why do you doubt?"
translations.idiomatic: 'Repent at evening what you did at morning, and the gentleman will approve'—why should you still doubt?

sentences.zh: 其專心一意毋怠，近醫藥以自持。」

translations.literal: Give your single-minded heart and do not slacken; draw near physicians and drugs to sustain yourself."
translations.idiomatic: Give the matter your undivided attention and do not flag; stay close to physicians and medicine to keep your strength."

sentences.zh: 方進乃起視事，條奏長所厚善京兆尹孫寶、右扶風蕭育，刺史二千石以上免二十餘人，其見任如此。

translations.literal: Fangjin thereupon rose and attended to affairs; in itemized memorial he reported those whom Chang had thickly befriended—Metropolitan Governor Sun Bao, Administrator of the Right Xiao Yu—and regional inspectors and those at two-thousand-bushel rank and above, more than twenty persons dismissed; such was how he was trusted in appointment.
translations.idiomatic: Fangjin returned to duty and filed a detailed memorial naming everyone close to Chunyu Chang—among them Metropolitan Governor Sun Bao and Administrator of the Right Xiao Yu—getting more than twenty regional inspectors and senior two-thousand-bushel officials removed. Such was the confidence the throne placed in him.

sentences.zh: 方進雖受《穀梁》，然好《左氏傳》、天文星曆，其《左氏》則國師劉歆，星曆則長安令田終術師也。

translations.literal: Although Fangjin had received the "Guliang" commentary, yet he loved the "Zuo Tradition", astronomy and star calendars; for the "Zuo Tradition" his teacher was State Teacher Liu Xin; for star calendars his teacher was Chang'an magistrate Tian Zhongshu.
translations.idiomatic: Though trained in the "Guliang" commentary, he favored the "Zuo Tradition" and astrology; for the former his master was State Teacher Liu Xin, for celestial calculations Chang'an Magistrate Tian Zhongshu.

sentences.zh: 厚李尋，以為議曹。

translations.literal: He treated Li Xun generously and made him deliberation clerk.
translations.idiomatic: He favored Li Xun and appointed him to his deliberation staff.

sentences.zh: 為相九歲，綏和二年春熒惑守心，尋奏記言：「應變之權，君侯所自明。

translations.literal: Having been chancellor nine years, in the spring of the second year of Suihe Mars guarded the Heart; Xun submitted a written note saying: "The authority to respond to change is what you, lord, yourself understand clearly.
translations.idiomatic: In his ninth year as chief minister, in the spring of Suihe 2, Mars stationed in the Heart constellation. Li Xun sent a private note: "The art of adapting to portents is something you, my lord, understand without being told.

sentences.zh: 往者數白，三光垂象，變動見端，山川水泉，反理視患，民人訛謠，斥事感名。

translations.literal: Formerly repeatedly it was reported white; the three luminaries hung signs; change and motion showed beginnings; mountains and rivers and springs, contrary to pattern showed calamity; the people spread false rumors; events matched names.
translations.idiomatic: Time and again the heavens have flashed warnings: the three lights have shown strange aspects, anomalies have appeared in land and water, and popular rumor has linked events to names.

sentences.zh: 三者既效，可為寒心。

translations.literal: The three kinds having already proved effective, one may feel cold in the heart.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven, earth, and rumor have already borne witness; the prospect should chill any thoughtful man.

sentences.zh: 今提揚眉，矢貫中，狼奮角，弓且張，金歷庫，士逆度，輔湛沒，火守舍，萬歲之期，近慎朝暮。

translations.literal: Now the Dipper raises its brow, the arrow pierces the center, the Wolf exerts its horn, the bow is about to be drawn, metal passes through the granary, the knight opposes the measure, the assistant sinks and is lost, fire guards the lodge—the term of ten thousand years draws near; be careful morning and evening.
translations.idiomatic: The sky now shows Mars at the Heart, the Bow drawn taut, celestial metal threatening the arsenal, guardian stars displaced, and the Red Planet lodged in the ruler's seat: the fateful moment may fall as soon as tomorrow morning or tomorrow night.

sentences.zh: 上無惻怛濟世之功，下無推讓避賢之效，欲當大位，為具臣以全身，難矣！

translations.literal: Above there is no compassionate work of aiding the age; below there is no effect of yielding and declining for the worthy; wishing to occupy the great position, to be a mere vessel minister and preserve the whole person—this is hard!
translations.idiomatic: You have shown the world neither compassionate statesmanship nor the humility to yield to worthier men. To cling to the highest office while playing the cipher who merely saves his skin will not be easy.

sentences.zh: 大責日加，安得但保斥逐之戮？

translations.literal: Great blame daily increases; how can one merely preserve the punishment of dismissal and banishment?
translations.idiomatic: Blame from above mounts by the day; do you imagine simple removal from office will be the worst you face?

sentences.zh: 闔府三百餘人，唯君侯擇其中，與盡節轉凶。」

translations.literal: The whole office is more than three hundred persons; only you, lord, choose among them those who with you will exhaust fidelity and turn away ill fortune."
translations.idiomatic: More than three hundred men serve in your bureau—choose from them allies who will stake everything to help you turn this omen aside."

sentences.zh: 方進憂之，不知所出。

translations.literal: Fangjin worried over it and did not know what way out to take.
translations.idiomatic: Fangjin was deeply troubled but could see no way out.

sentences.zh: 會郎賁麗善為星，言大臣宜當之。

translations.literal: It happened that Gentleman Ben Li was good at stars and said a great minister ought to answer for it.
translations.idiomatic: Then Palace Gentleman Ben Li, an adept at astrology, declared that a senior minister must bear the omen's weight.

sentences.zh: 上乃召見方進。

translations.literal: The emperor thereupon summoned and saw Fangjin.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor summoned Fangjin to audience.

sentences.zh: 還歸，未及引決，上遂賜冊曰：「皇帝問丞相：君孔子之慮，孟賁之勇，朕嘉與君同心一意，庶幾有成。

translations.literal: He returned home; before he had yet reached drawing the knife in resolution, the emperor thereupon bestowed a document, saying: The emperor inquires of the Chancellor: you have Confucius's deliberation, Meng Ben's courage; I rejoice that with you we are of one heart and one mind, and hope there may be accomplishment.
translations.idiomatic: Fangjin went home; before he could act on his thoughts of suicide, the emperor's written message arrived: "The emperor addresses the Chancellor: you combine the prudence of Confucius with the daring of Meng Ben. I have valued our single purpose; I had hoped we would finish the work together.

sentences.zh: 惟君登位，於今十年，災害並臻，民被飢餓，加以疾疫溺死，關門牡開，失國守備，盜賊黨輩。

translations.literal: Since you ascended the post, down to today ten years, disasters have arrived together; the people have been struck by hunger; in addition there are epidemic and drowning death; the passes' bolts have been opened; state guard and defense have been lost; bandits and factional bands.
translations.idiomatic: Yet in the ten years since you took office calamities have piled up: famine, plague, drownings, frontier gates left unbarred, garrisons neglected, and bandit gangs on the loose.

sentences.zh: 吏民殘賊，毆殺良民，斷獄歲歲多前。

translations.literal: Officials and people are cruel and violent, beat and kill good commoners; cases judged year by year exceed those before.
translations.idiomatic: Officials and commoners alike turn predatory, beating and killing the innocent, and each year the docket of lawsuits grows heavier than the last.

sentences.zh: 上書言事，交錯道路，懷奸朋黨，相為隱蔽，皆亡忠慮，群下凶凶，更相嫉妒，其咎安在？

translations.literal: Those who submit memorials reporting affairs crisscross the roads; harboring wickedness they form factions, mutually conceal one another; all lack loyal thought; the throng below are violent, mutually envious—where does the blame lie?
translations.idiomatic: Petitions choke the highways; men nurse private schemes, band into cliques, and cover for one another—nowhere do I see loyal counsel, only snarling rivalry below. Where does fault lie if not with the chief minister?

sentences.zh: 觀君之治，無慾輔朕富民便安元元之念。

translations.literal: Observing your governance, there is no thought of aiding Us to enrich the people and bring convenience and security to the multitude.
translations.idiomatic: In your administration I find no zeal to help me enrich the people and settle the common folk.

sentences.zh: 間者郡國谷雖頗熟，百姓不足者尚眾，前去城郭，未能盡還，夙夜未嘗忘焉。

translations.literal: Recently though commanderies and kingdoms' grain has been fairly ripe, of the hundred surnames those insufficient are still many; those who earlier left the walls have not been able entirely to return—We have never forgotten it morning and night.
translations.idiomatic: Lately the harvests have been fair in places, yet vast numbers of households still lack food; refugees who fled the cities have not all returned—that weighs on me day and night.

sentences.zh: 朕惟往時之用，與今一也，百僚用度各有數。

translations.literal: We consider that the uses of former times and today are one; the hundred officials' expenditures each have a reckoning.
translations.idiomatic: State outlays today are no different from what they were; every ministry has a fixed budget.

sentences.zh: 君有量多少，一聽群下言，用度不足，奏請一切增賦，稅城郭□及園田，過更，算馬牛羊，增益鹽鐵，變更無常。

translations.literal: You had measure of more or less, wholly listened to the throng below's words; when expenditures were insufficient, you memorialized requesting blanket increase of levies, taxed city walls □ and garden fields, relay service, assessed horses, cattle, and sheep, increased and augmented salt and iron, changes without constancy.
translations.idiomatic: You gauged revenue by whatever subordinates suggested; whenever funds ran short you ordered sweeping new imposts—taxes on urban fortifications and garden plots, service levies, head taxes on livestock, repeated hikes to the salt and iron monopolies—swinging policy this way and that without principle.

sentences.zh: 朕既不明，隨奏許可，後議者以為不便，制詔下君，君雲賣酒醪。

translations.literal: We being not clear, followed memorials and granted approval; afterward deliberators thought it inconvenient; a regulated edict was issued down to you, and you reported selling ale mash.
translations.idiomatic: Too readily I approved your requests; when critics protested, I issued edicts to rein you in, and you answered by proposing to sell government ale.

sentences.zh: 後請止，未盡月復奏議令賣酒醪。

translations.literal: Afterward you requested to stop; not yet a full month again you memorialized arguing to order sale of ale mash.
translations.idiomatic: You then asked to halt the scheme, yet before a month had passed you were back with another memorial urging the ale monopoly again.

sentences.zh: 朕誠怪君，何持容容之計，無忠固意，將何以輔朕帥道群下？

translations.literal: We truly marvel at you—why do you hold a accommodating plan, with no loyal firm intent? How will you aid Us and lead and guide the throng below?
translations.idiomatic: I am baffled by this drift and evasion—where is the steadfast loyalty with which you should guide me and lead the bureaucracy?

sentences.zh: 而欲久蒙顯尊之位，豈不難哉！

translations.literal: Yet you wish long to cover yourself with the illustrious exalted position—is it not difficult!
translations.idiomatic: Do you imagine you can long hold the loftiest post on such terms?

sentences.zh: 傳曰：『高而不危，所以長守貴也。』

translations.literal: The tradition says: "High yet not perilous—thereby one long keeps nobility."
translations.idiomatic: The classic says, "Stand high without tottering, and you may keep rank for good."

sentences.zh: 欲退君位，尚未忍。

translations.literal: I wish to dismiss you from position, but have not yet been able to bear it.
translations.idiomatic: I would remove you, yet I cannot yet bring myself to do it.

sentences.zh: 君其孰念詳計，塞絕奸原，憂國如家，務便百姓以輔朕。

translations.literal: You should ponder carefully, plan in detail, block and cut off the fount of wickedness, worry for the state as for a family, strive to benefit the hundred surnames in order to aid Us.
translations.idiomatic: Think this through, choke off every source of abuse, love the realm as your own household, and bend every effort to ease the people and support your sovereign.

sentences.zh: 朕既已改，君其自思，強食慎職。

translations.literal: We having already changed course, you should yourself reflect; strengthen food and be careful in duty.
translations.idiomatic: I have reconsidered my course; search your own heart, eat well, and attend scrupulously to your duties.

sentences.zh: 使尚書令賜君上尊酒十石，養牛一，君審外焉。」

translations.literal: We order the Supervisor of the Household to bestow on you ten piculs of finest ale and one fattening ox; you examine what is outside."
translations.idiomatic: The Supervisor of the Household is directed to bring you ten piculs of finest wine and one fattening ox—you know what such an imperial gift portends."

sentences.zh: 方進即日自殺。

translations.literal: Fangjin that day killed himself.
translations.idiomatic: Fangjin took his own life that same day.

sentences.zh: 上秘之，遣九卿冊贈以丞相、高陵侯印綬，賜乘輿秘器，少府供張，柱檻皆衣素。

translations.literal: The emperor kept it secret; he sent the nine ministers with document to confer posthumously the chancellor and Marquis of Gaoling seals and ribbons, bestowed imperial secret coffin goods, the Privy Treasury supplied hangings, pillars and balustrades were all clothed in plain white.
translations.idiomatic: The death was hushed up while the nine ministers were sent with patent cords naming him posthumously chancellor and Marquis of Gaoling, with imperial-grade burial gear from the Privy Treasury and hangings of unbleached cloth draping every pillar and rail.

sentences.zh: 天子親臨吊者數至，禮賜異於它相故事。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven personally attended condolence visits several times; ritual gifts differed from precedent for other chancellors.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor called repeatedly in person to mourn, with honors that exceeded the usual treatment of chief ministers.

sentences.zh: 謚曰恭侯。

translations.literal: His posthumous title was called Reverent Marquis.
translations.idiomatic: He was given the posthumous title Reverent Marquis.

sentences.zh: 長子宣嗣。

translations.literal: The eldest son Xuan succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: The eldest son, Xuan, inherited the title.

sentences.zh: 宣字少伯，亦明經篤行，君子人也。

translations.literal: Xuan, courtesy name Shaobo, also understood the classics and was earnest in conduct—a gentleman person.
translations.idiomatic: Zhai Xuan, styled Shaobo, was classically trained, upright in conduct, and accounted a true gentleman.

sentences.zh: 及方進在，為關都尉、南郡太守。

translations.literal: While Fangjin was alive, he became Pass Commandant and grand warden of Nan commandery.
translations.idiomatic: During his father's lifetime he served as Pass Commandant and as grand warden of Nan commandery.

sentences.zh: 少子曰義。

translations.literal: The younger son was named Yi.
translations.idiomatic: The younger son was Yi.

sentences.zh: 義字文仲，少以父任為郎，稍遷諸曹，年二十出為南陽都尉。

translations.literal: Yi, courtesy name Wenzhong, in youth by father's privilege became gentleman, gradually was transferred among the various bureaus, at age twenty went out as chief commandant of Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Zhai Yi, styled Wenzhong, entered the palace corps by his father's rank, rose through several offices, and at twenty was posted chief commandant of Nanyang.

sentences.zh: 宛令劉立與曲陽侯為婚，又素著名州郡，輕義年少。

translations.literal: Wan magistrate Liu Li was affined by marriage to Marquis of Quyang, and also by nature was famed in commanderies and states; he treated Yi lightly for youth.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Li of Wan was kin by marriage to the Marquis of Quyang and enjoyed wide renown in the region; he despised Yi for his youth.

sentences.zh: 義行太守事，行縣至宛，丞相史在傳捨。

translations.literal: Yi acted grand warden's affairs, on circuit reached Wan; a chancellor's clerk was at the relay inn.
translations.idiomatic: While acting as grand warden, Yi made his rounds to Wan, where a chancellery clerk was staying at the post station.

sentences.zh: 立持酒餚謁丞相史，對飲未訖，會義亦往，外吏白都尉方至，立語言身若。

translations.literal: Li carried wine and dishes to visit the chancellor's clerk; facing each other drinking not yet finished, it happened Yi also went; an outer clerk reported the chief commandant was about to arrive; Li's speech and body were as if unconcerned.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Li brought wine and food to entertain the clerk; they were still drinking when Yi arrived. A runner announced that the chief commandant was coming, yet Liu continued chatting as if nothing mattered.

sentences.zh: 須臾義至，內謁徑入，立乃走下。

translations.literal: In a moment Yi arrived, inner announcement went straight in, Li thereupon ran down.
translations.idiomatic: Moments later Yi strode in without ceremony, and Liu Li scrambled down from his seat.

sentences.zh: 義既還，大怒，陽以他事召立至，以主守盜十金，賊殺不辜，部掾夏恢等收縛立，傳送鄧獄。

translations.literal: After Yi returned, greatly angry, on pretext of another matter summoned Li to come, charged him with guarding and stealing ten pounds of gold, bandit-killing the guiltless; department clerk Xia Hui and others seized and bound Li and transmitted him to Deng prison.
translations.idiomatic: Back at headquarters Yi was furious. He summoned Liu on other pretexts, then had him arrested for embezzling ten catties of public gold and murdering innocents; clerks Xia Hui and others bound him and sent him under escort to the jail at Deng.

sentences.zh: 恢亦以宛大縣，恐見篡奪，白義可因隨後行縣送鄧。

translations.literal: Hui also because Wan was a great county, feared seeing seizure and usurpation, reported to Yi that he could follow afterward on circuit through counties and send to Deng.
translations.idiomatic: Xia Hui, knowing Wan was a powerful county and fearing a rescue attempt, advised Yi to escort the prisoner himself on the tour and onward to Deng.

sentences.zh: 義曰：「欲令都尉自送，則如勿收邪？」

translations.literal: Yi said: "If you wish to make the chief commandant personally escort, then is it as if not having arrested him?"
translations.idiomatic: Yi retorted, "Shall the chief commandant play courier? If I must march him there myself, why bother to arrest him at all?"

sentences.zh: 載環宛市乃送，吏民不敢動，威震南陽。

translations.literal: They loaded him and circled Wan market then sent him; officials and people did not dare move; awe shook Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: He had the cage cart circle the entire market of Wan before the escort left; officials and townsfolk dared not stir—his authority stunned the whole commandery.

sentences.zh: 立家輕騎馳從武關入語曲陽侯，曲陽侯白成帝，帝以問丞相。

translations.literal: Li's family light cavalry galloped from Wu Pass entering to speak to Marquis of Quyang; Marquis of Quyang reported to Emperor Cheng; the emperor thereby asked the chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: Liu's kinsmen raced light horsemen through Wu Pass to alert the Marquis of Quyang, who carried the matter to Emperor Cheng; the emperor turned to the chancellor for guidance.

sentences.zh: 方進遣吏敕義出宛令。

translations.literal: Fangjin sent a clerk ordering Yi to release the Wan magistrate.
translations.idiomatic: Fangjin dispatched an order directing Yi to release the magistrate of Wan.

sentences.zh: 宛令已出，吏還白狀。

translations.literal: The Wan magistrate already having been released, the clerk returned and reported the situation.
translations.idiomatic: After Liu was freed, the clerk reported back.

sentences.zh: 方進曰：「小兒未知為吏也，其意以為入獄當輒死矣。」

translations.literal: Fangjin said: "The young lad does not yet know how to be an official; in his idea he thought that entering prison ought always to mean death."
translations.idiomatic: Fangjin remarked, "The boy still has no idea how government works; he thinks everyone thrown in jail dies at once."

sentences.zh: 後義坐法免，起家而為弘農太守，遷河內太守、青州牧。

translations.literal: Afterward Yi sat by law and was dismissed; rising from house he became grand warden of Hongnong, was transferred to grand warden of Henei and shepherd of Qingzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Yi later lost office for a legal fault, then was recalled from commoner status as grand warden of Hongnong, promoted to Henei, and named regional shepherd of Qingzhou.

sentences.zh: 所居著名，有父風烈。

translations.literal: Wherever he dwelt he was famed; he had his father's wind and fierceness.
translations.idiomatic: Wherever he served he made a name for himself, in the same stern mold as his father.

sentences.zh: 徙為東郡太守。

translations.literal: He was transferred to be grand warden of Dong commandery.
translations.idiomatic: He was then transferred to grand warden of Dong commandery.

sentences.zh: 數歲，平帝崩，王莽居攝，義心惡之，乃謂姊子上蔡陳豐曰：「新都侯攝天子位，號令天下，故擇宗室幼稚者以為孺子，依托周公輔成王之義，且以觀望，必代漢家，其漸可見。

translations.literal: After several years, Emperor Ping died; Wang Mang acted as regent; Yi's heart hated it; he thereupon said to his elder sister's son Chen Feng of Shangcai: "The Marquis of Xindu holds the Son of Heaven's position and commands the realm; therefore he chose an imperial child of tender years to be the infant heir, relying on the Duke of Zhou's aiding King Cheng's meaning, and meanwhile to watch and wait—he will certainly replace the Han house; the gradual process is visible.
translations.idiomatic: A few years later Emperor Ping died and Wang Mang assumed the regency. Yi loathed it and said to his sister's son Chen Feng of Shangcai: "The Marquis of Xindu has seized the throne in all but name. He picked a toddler from the Liu clan as puppet heir, invoking the Duke of Zhou and the boy King Cheng while he bides his time. He means to supplant the Han; the slope is already plain to see."

sentences.zh: 方今宗室衰弱，外無強蕃，天下傾首服從，莫能亢扞國難。

translations.literal: Now the imperial house is weak and declining; outside there are no strong feudatories; the realm bows head and submits; none can resist and ward off the state's calamity.
translations.idiomatic: The imperial clan is enfeebled, no powerful feudatories ring the capital, and the empire bows its neck to Mang—no one stands ready to bear the crisis for the dynasty.

sentences.zh: 吾幸得備宰相子，身守大郡，父子受漢厚恩，義當為國討賊，以安社稷。

translations.literal: I fortunately was able to fill the chancellor's son's place; my person guards a great commandery; father and son received Han's thick grace; in righteousness I ought for the state to punish rebels, thereby to secure altars of soil and grain.
translations.idiomatic: I am the son of a chief minister and command a great commandery; my father and I owe the Han everything. It is my duty to raise arms against this traitor and secure the altars of state.

sentences.zh: 欲舉兵西誅不當攝者，選宗室子孫輔而立之。

translations.literal: I wish to raise troops westward to execute the one who ought not to act as regent, select imperial descendants to assist and establish him.
translations.idiomatic: I mean to march west, kill the usurper, and set up a worthy Liu scion with loyal ministers at his side.

sentences.zh: 設令時命不成，死國埋名，猶可以不漸於先帝。

translations.literal: Suppose the time's mandate is not accomplished; dying for the state and burying the name, still one may thereby not disgrace the former emperors.
translations.idiomatic: If fate denies us victory, I will die for the Han and leave a clean name—better than shaming the late emperors.

sentences.zh: 今欲發之，乃肯從我乎？」

translations.literal: Now I wish to launch it—will you consent to follow me?"
translations.idiomatic: I am ready to move—will you follow me?"

sentences.zh: 豐年十八，勇壯，許諾。

translations.literal: Feng was eighteen years old, brave and stalwart, and promised.
translations.idiomatic: Feng was eighteen, bold and strong, and swore to join him.

sentences.zh: 義遂與東郡都尉劉宇、嚴鄉侯劉信、信弟武平侯劉璜結謀。

translations.literal: Yi thereupon with Dong commandery chief commandant Liu Yu, Marquis of Yanxiang Liu Xin, and Xin's younger brother Marquis of Wuping Liu Huang knotted plot.
translations.idiomatic: Yi then conspired with Dong commandery Chief Commandant Liu Yu, Marquis of Yanxiang Liu Xin, and Xin's younger brother Marquis of Wuping Liu Huang.

sentences.zh: 及車郡王孫慶素有勇略，以明兵法，征在京師，義乃詐移書以重罪傳逮慶。

translations.literal: Moreover Wang Sunqing of Che commandery by nature had courage and strategy, by understanding military methods was summoned to the capital; Yi thereupon falsely circulated documents with heavy crime to transmit and seize Qing.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Sunqing of Che commandery—a seasoned strategist summoned to the capital for his military expertise—was trapped when Yi circulated a forged warrant accusing him of a capital crime and had him seized.

sentences.zh: 於是以九月都試日斬觀令，因勒其車騎材官士，募郡中勇敢，部署將帥。

translations.literal: Thereupon on the ninth month's capital drill day he beheaded the magistrate of Guan, thereby mustered his chariots, cavalry, specialist archer soldiers, recruited the commandery's brave, and deployed generals and commanders.
translations.idiomatic: On the autumn muster day in the ninth month he executed the magistrate of Guan, seized the commandery's chariotry, cavalry, and crossbowmen, enlisted every bold man in the district, and appointed his generals.

sentences.zh: 嚴鄉侯信者，東平王雲子也。

translations.literal: Marquis of Yanxiang Xin was the son of King of Dongping Yun.
translations.idiomatic: Marquis of Yanxiang Liu Xin was a son of Liu Yun, king of Dongping.

sentences.zh: 雲誅死，信兄開明嗣為王，薨，無子，而信子匡復立為王，故義舉兵並東平，立信為天子。

translations.literal: Yun was executed and died; Xin's elder brother Kaiming succeeded as king, died, had no son, yet Xin's son Kuang again was established as king; therefore Yi raised troops and annexed Dongping, established Xin as Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: After Liu Yun was executed, Xin's brother Kaiming inherited the kingdom but died without an heir; Xin's son Kuang was then enthroned as king. Yi therefore marched into Dongping, absorbed its forces, and proclaimed Liu Xin emperor.

sentences.zh: 義自號大司馬柱天大將軍，以東平王傅蘇隆為丞相，中尉皋丹為御史大夫，移檄郡國，言莽鴆殺孝平皇帝，矯攝尊號，今天子已立，共行天罰。

translations.literal: Yi styled himself grand marshal and pillar-heaven great general; he made tutor to the king of Dongping Su Long chancellor, commandant of the guards Gao Dan imperial counselor; he circulated proclamations to commanderies and kingdoms, saying Mang poison-killed Emperor Xiaoping, falsely assumed regent's honored title, now the Son of Heaven has been established—together carry out Heaven's punishment.
translations.idiomatic: Yi declared himself grand marshal and "pillar of heaven" generalissimo, named Su Long, the former tutor to the king of Dongping, as his chancellor, and Palace Commandant Gao Dan as imperial counselor. His manifestos accused Wang Mang of poisoning Emperor Ping, usurping the regency, and proclaimed that a true Son of Heaven now stood with them to execute Heaven's judgment.

sentences.zh: 郡國皆震，比至山陽，眾十餘萬。

translations.literal: Commanderies and kingdoms all were shaken; by the time he reached Shanyang, the host was more than a hundred thousand.
translations.idiomatic: The provinces were rocked; by the time he reached Shanyang his army exceeded one hundred thousand.

sentences.zh: 莽聞之，大懼，乃拜其黨親輕車將軍成武侯孫建為奮武將軍，光祿勳成都侯王邑為虎牙將軍，明義侯王駿為強弩將軍，春王城門校尉王況為震威將軍，宗伯忠孝侯劉宏為奮衝將軍，中少府建威侯王昌為中堅將軍，中郎將震羌侯竇兄為奮威將軍，凡七人，自擇除關西人為校尉軍吏，將關東甲卒，發奔命以擊義焉。

translations.literal: When Mang heard it, he was greatly afraid; thereupon he appointed his faction and kin—Light Chariot general Marquis of Chengwu Sun Jian as Raising Martial general, Superintendent of the Household Marquis of Chengdu Wang Yi as Tiger Fang general, Marquis of Mingyi Wang Jun as Strong Crossbow general, Spring Gate Commandant of the Gates Wang Kuang as Shaking Awe general, Minister of the Imperial Clan Marquis of Loyal Filial Piety Liu Hong as Raising Assault general, Privy Treasurer Jianwei Marquis Wang Chang as Center Hard general, Gentleman of the Palace Marquis Who Shakes the Qiang Dou Xiong as Raising Might general—in all seven men; he himself chose and appointed men from west of the passes as colonels and army clerks, led east-of-the-pass armored soldiers, and issued emergency call-ups to strike Yi.
translations.idiomatic: Mang was terrified. He commissioned seven kinsmen and allies: Sun Jian, Marquis of Chengwu, as Raising Martial general; Wang Yi, Marquis of Chengdu, as Tiger Fang general; Wang Jun, Marquis of Mingyi, as Strong Crossbow general; Wang Kuang, commandant of the Chunwang Gate, as Shaking Awe general; Liu Hong, Marquis of Loyal Filial Piety, as Raising Assault general; Wang Chang, Marquis of Jianwei, as Center Hard general; and Dou Xiong, Marquis Who Shakes the Qiang, as Raising Might general. Mang personally picked officers from west of the passes as colonels and staff, mobilized armored troops from the east, and called out every emergency levy to crush Yi.

sentences.zh: 復以太僕武讓為積弩將軍屯函谷關，將作大匠蒙鄉侯逯並為橫野將軍屯武關，羲和紅休侯劉歆為揚武將軍屯宛，太保後丞丞陽侯甄邯為大將軍屯霸上，常鄉侯王惲為車騎將軍屯平樂館，騎都尉王晏為建威將軍屯城北，城門校尉趙恢為城門將軍，皆勒兵自奮。

translations.literal: Again he made Grand Coachman Wu Rang Piled Crossbow general to garrison Hangu Pass, Court Architect Marquis of Mengxiang Lu Bing Transverse Wild general to garrison Wu Pass, Minister of the Calendar Hongxiu Marquis Liu Xin General Who Displays Martial Prowess to garrison Wan, Grand Guardian and Rear Chancellor Marquis of Chengyang Zhen Han grand general to garrison Bashang, Marquis of Changxiang Wang Yun chariot and cavalry general to garrison Pingle Lodge, Cavalry Commandant Wang Yan Establishing Might general to garrison north of the city, Commandant of the Gates Zhao Hui Gate general—all tightened troops and roused themselves.
translations.idiomatic: He also named Grand Coachman Wu Rang Piled Crossbow general at Hangu Pass; Court Architect Lu Bing, Marquis of Mengxiang, as Transverse Wild general at Wu Pass; Liu Xin, Hongxiu marquis and minister of the calendar, as general displaying martial prowess at Nanyang; Zhen Han, Marquis of Chengyang, as grand general at Bashang; Wang Yun, Marquis of Changxiang, as chariot-and-cavalry general at Pingle Lodge; Cavalry Commandant Wang Yan as Establishing Might general north of the walls; and Gate Commandant Zhao Hui as gate general. Each braced his command for battle.

sentences.zh: 莽日抱孺子會群臣而稱曰：「昔成王幼，周公攝政，而管、蔡挾祿父以畔，今翟義亦挾劉信而作亂。

translations.literal: Mang daily embraced the infant heir, met the various ministers, and declared, saying: "Anciently when King Cheng was young, the Duke of Zhou acted as regent, yet Guan and Cai clasped Father Lu and rebelled; now Zhai Yi also clasps Liu Xin and raises rebellion."
translations.idiomatic: Day after day Wang Mang held the boy emperor before the court and declared: "When King Cheng was a child, the Duke of Zhou ruled in his stead—yet Guan Shu and Cai Shu rallied Prince Lu Fu and rose in revolt. Zhai Yi has done the same, hiding behind Liu Xin."

sentences.zh: 自古大聖猶懼此，況臣莽之鬥筲！」

translations.literal: Since antiquity even the great sages still feared this—how much more minister Mang's dipper-and-scoop!"
translations.idiomatic: The greatest sages of old dreaded such moments—what of a mere measure like your servant Mang!"

sentences.zh: 群臣皆曰：「不遭此變，不章聖德。」

translations.literal: The various ministers all said: "Had we not encountered this change, the sage virtue would not have been displayed."
translations.idiomatic: The ministers chorused, "Without this crisis, the sage virtue of the regency would never have shone forth."

sentences.zh: 莽於是依《周書》作《大誥》，曰：

translations.literal: Mang thereupon according to the "Documents of Zhou" composed the "Great Announcement", saying:
translations.idiomatic: Mang then modeled a "Great Announcement" on the "Documents of Zhou" and proclaimed:

sentences.zh: 惟居攝二年十月甲子，攝皇帝若曰：大誥道諸侯王、三公、列侯於汝卿、大夫、元士御事。

translations.literal: It being the second year of acting regency, tenth month, day jiazi, the Acting Emperor speaks thus: the Great Announcement instructs the feudal kings, Three Dukes, and full marquises—and you ministers, great officers, and grandees charged with affairs.
translations.idiomatic: On the jiazi day of the tenth month in the second regency year, the Acting Emperor declared: "This Great Proclamation goes out to the kings, the Three Dukes, the full marquises—and to you ministers, senior officials, and all who handle the business of state."

sentences.zh: 不吊，天降喪於趙、傅、丁、董。

translations.literal: Heaven was not compassionate; Heaven sent down mourning on the Zhao, Fu, Ding, and Dong.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven showed no pity: it brought disaster on the houses of Zhao, Fu, Ding, and Dong.

sentences.zh: 洪惟我幼沖孺子，當承繼嗣無疆大曆服事，予未遭其明哲能道民於安，況其能往知天命！

translations.literal: Greatly do I consider our young tender infant; he ought to inherit the succession in the boundless great calendar and serve the tasks—I have not yet encountered his clear wisdom able to lead the people to peace, how much less can he go and know Heaven's mandate!
translations.idiomatic: I think of our infant heir who must inherit an endless mandate: I have not yet seen in him the wisdom to settle the people in peace, still less to read Heaven's will for himself.

sentences.zh: 熙！

translations.literal: Oh!
translations.idiomatic: Ah!

sentences.zh: 我念孺子，若涉淵水，予惟往求朕所濟度，奔走以傅近奉承高皇帝所受命，予豈敢自比於前人乎！

translations.literal: I think on the infant as if fording deep water; I only go seeking what I may ford across, running and hastening to draw near and receive what the August Emperor Gao received in mandate—how dare I compare myself to the men of old!
translations.idiomatic: I think of the child as one crossing deep water: I must find a way across, running to fulfill the charge that Gaozu received from Heaven—how dare I rank myself with the ancients!

sentences.zh: 天降威明，用寧帝室，遺我居攝寶龜。

translations.literal: Heaven sent down awesome clarity, thereby to quiet the imperial house, and left me the regency's precious tortoise.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven sent stern portents to steady the imperial house and entrusted me with the sacred tortoise of the regency.

sentences.zh: 太皇太后以丹石之符，乃紹天明意，詔予即命居攝踐祚，如周公故事。

translations.literal: The Grand Empress Dowager by cinnabar-and-stone talismans then continued Heaven's bright intent, edicting me to take up the mandate, act as regent, and tread the throne, according to the Duke of Zhou's precedent.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Empress Dowager, reading the red-stone portents, carried out Heaven's intent and ordered me to accept the mandate, rule as regent, and mount the throne after the Duke of Zhou's precedent.

sentences.zh: 反虜故東郡太守翟義擅興師動眾，曰「有大難於西土，西土人亦不靖。」

translations.literal: The rebel captive, former grand warden of Dong commandery Zhai Yi, on his own raised troops and moved the host, saying, "There is great calamity in the western lands; the western lands' people are also not quiet."
translations.idiomatic: The traitor Zhai Yi, erstwhile grand warden of Dong, raised an army and cried that "the west faces great disaster and its people are in turmoil."

sentences.zh: 於是動嚴鄉侯信，誕敢犯祖亂宗之序。

translations.literal: Thereby he moved Marquis of Yanxiang Xin, who greatly dared violate ancestors and disorder the temple's sequence.
translations.idiomatic: He incited Marquis of Yanxiang Liu Xin to outrage that overturns ancestral order and shames the imperial clan.

sentences.zh: 天降威遺我寶龜，固知我國有□災，使民不安，是天反覆右我漢國也。

translations.literal: Heaven sent down awe and left me the precious tortoise, thereby knowing our state has □ calamity, making the people not at peace—this is Heaven turning again to assist our Han state.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven sent terror and gave me the sacred tortoise, showing that our land suffers calamity and the people are restless—yet that is Heaven once more turning to aid the house of Han.

sentences.zh: 粵其聞日，宗室之俟有四百人，民獻儀九萬夫，予敬以終於此謀繼嗣圖功。

translations.literal: On the day it was heard, of the imperial clan's nobles there were four hundred persons; the people presented models, ninety thousand men; I respectfully thereby brought to completion this plan for succession and mapped merit.
translations.idiomatic: On the day the news came, four hundred Liu nobles and ninety thousand commoners who offered counsel rallied to me; I have earnestly pursued this plan to secure the succession and achieve the task.

sentences.zh: 我有大事，休，予卜並吉，故我出大將告郡太守、諸侯相、令、長曰：「予得吉卜，予惟以汝於伐東郡嚴鄉逋播臣。」

translations.literal: I have a great affair, auspicious; I divined and all was auspicious; therefore I issued great generals informing grand wardens, kingdom chancellors, magistrates, and chiefs, saying: "I obtained auspicious divination; I therefore use you to attack the scattered and fleeing ministers of Yanxiang in Dong commandery."
translations.idiomatic: This is a great enterprise, and the tortoise-shell has given only favorable signs. I have therefore sent my generals to tell every grand warden, kingdom chancellor, and local magistrate: "The oracle is good; I charge you to march against the renegades of Yanxiang in Dong commandery."

sentences.zh: 爾國君或者無不反曰：「難大，民亦不靜，亦惟在帝官諸侯宗室，於小子族父，敬不可征。」

translations.literal: You state rulers perhaps none did not reply, saying: "The hardship is great; the people are also not quiet; it lies only with the emperor's officials, feudal lords, and imperial clan—toward your servant's junior and clan uncles—reverently we cannot punish."
translations.idiomatic: Some of you rulers answered, "The peril is great and the people uneasy; the fault lies with palace officials, feudal lords, and kinsmen of the throne—surely we must not raise arms against your servant's uncles."

sentences.zh: 帝不違卜，故予為沖人長思厥難曰：「嗚呼！

translations.literal: The emperor did not oppose divination; therefore I, being a minor person, long pondered its hardship, saying: "Alas!
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven did not reject the oracle; I, a mere child in office, have brooded long on this crisis and cry, "Alas!"

sentences.zh: 義、信所犯，誠動鰥寡，哀哉！」

translations.literal: What Yi and Xin committed truly moves widowers and widows—how pitiful!"
translations.idiomatic: The crimes of Yi and Xin stir pity even in widows and widowers—how lamentable!"

sentences.zh: 予遭天役遺，大解難於予身，以為孺子，不身自恤。
